rationale: The cited paper is used in the theory section to define self-focused attention and to support the claim that self-focus increases reliance on internal, self-referential cues in decision making, which is central to the moderator logic of H3. It is also cited in the methods section as prior work for a self-focus sentence-construction measure used in Study 3. Although grouped with other references, its role goes beyond a passing background mention because it helps justify both conceptual setup and operationalization of a key construct.
relation_description: The citing paper builds its self-focus moderation argument partly on Chang and Hung's evidence that self-focused attention shifts how consumers rely on internal cues, and it uses that stream to motivate identity-consistent SEP preferences under high self-focus. It also draws on the cited work as methodological precedent for measuring self-focus in an experiment.
